guests.name: Hilda kolektiv
guests.description: Hilda (*2022) is a collective of artists formed out of friendship, whose practice is based on the principles of collaborative work and polyphonic creation. Their collaborative work is defined by an emphasis on process, mutual influence, and respect for the diverse perspectives and experiences of each member. The collective does not strive for a unified aesthetic, but rather for a layered interconnection of approaches that take shape through dialogue and collaborative situations. A fundamental role in their work is played by the effort to articulate contemporary experience through materials, forms, and images that carry memory and affective charge. Inspiration from the figure of Hildegard of Bingen frames their interest in synthetic modes of cognition that interconnect spiritual, visual, and environmental dimensions. The collective actively participates in exhibition and creative projects and undertakes joint artist residencies. Its members explore and experiment with what it means to be and create together – what forms collaborative work, shared responsibility, and the constant reevaluation of the collective’s very functioning can take.

guests.name: Kěř press
guests.description: Publishing grafted literature & seeding chaos. Kěř Press is a newly founded DIY publishing house; we organize Bookjam workshops and print zines and graphics. We’re interested in experiments and playing with words, text, and images. We love creating together, typesetting, and remixing. Slowly nurtured by Jakub Klimeš and Pája Smékalová. Ústí nad Labem and Prague.

guests.name: tomáš staněk a nora třísková
guests.description:
Urban stilllifes, wilderness and landscape in photography (nora) and illustration (tomáš), pragensie and all sorts of bushes and křáks. we have several zines, riso and digitally printed, some risographics and posters, one or two authors books.

@tms.stnk1 @nora.tri
